title: Tour the repo like a maintainer
objective: Map the repository into product docs, source modules, tests, scripts, and completion gates.
summary: This lesson turns the repo into a navigable map: package wiring, executable entry point, SandCore modules, matching tests, documentation sources, and local gates. The maintainer habit is to start from the behavior, choose the owning source area and test file, then verify with the right command instead of browsing randomly.

beats.title: Tour by responsibility, not by wandering
beats.text: A maintainer does not open random files until something looks relevant. Start with the user-facing behavior, name it using the Sandbox VM vocabulary from CONTEXT, then pick one likely source area and one likely test file. The repo is set up for that: docs explain the product contract, Sources/SandCore is split by responsibility, and Tests/SandCoreTests mirrors behavior areas. This lesson is a map, not a line-by-line source reading.

beats.title: Package.swift tells you the build shape
beats.text: Package.swift defines the project as a Swift package named sand. It exposes an executable product named sand from the executable target Sources/sand, a library product named SandCore from Sources/SandCore, and a test target named SandCoreTests under Tests/SandCoreTests. That means most behavior lives in SandCore, the CLI binary composes and runs it, and tests target the library rather than shelling through the installed command for every case.

beats.prompt: A command behavior changes. Which Package.swift target usually owns the implementation behavior?

choices.text: The sand executable target, because every command starts at the binary entry point.
The SandCore library target, with SandCoreTests covering the behavior.
The generated CLI reference, because it documents command behavior.

beats.feedback: The executable is important, but most maintainable behavior belongs in SandCore and its tests. Generated docs describe current behavior; they are not the first implementation stop.

beats.title: main.swift is the composition root
beats.text: Sources/sand/main.swift is intentionally small. It creates FileHostMetadataStore, AppleContainerCLIBackend, LifecycleCoordinator, and CLICommandRouter, then dispatches command-line arguments and exits with CommandResult.processExitCode. Read it to see what concrete pieces are wired together at runtime. Do not expect it to explain folder policy, spec parsing, lifecycle rules, or backend behavior; those live in SandCore modules.

beats.title: SandCore neighborhoods
beats.text: Sources/SandCore is organized by responsibility. CLI parses command shapes and request structs. Lifecycle coordinates create, apply, start, stop, run, shell, logs, status, spec, and folder mutations. Backend hides adapter details. Spec models Sandbox Specs. FolderPolicy owns Allowed Folder rules. WorkingDirectory maps Host Mac cwd to Guest Paths. Metadata stores Host Metadata. Ephemeral owns bounded Ephemeral Sandbox Runs. Doctor checks prerequisites. Domain holds small domain types. Status and Prompt handle presentation boundaries.

beats.prompt: Which source/test pairings are good first stops for the described behavior?

choices.text: Allowed Folder duplicate Guest Path rejection: Sources/SandCore/FolderPolicy/ and Tests/SandCoreTests/FolderPolicyTests.swift.
Command shape and unsupported v1 command routing: Sources/SandCore/CLI/ and Tests/SandCoreTests/CLICommandRouterTests.swift.
Working Directory Mapping fallback: Sources/SandCore/WorkingDirectory/ and Tests/SandCoreTests/WorkingDirectoryMapperTests.swift.
Generated CLI wording bug: edit docs/cli-reference.md by hand first, then look for tests later.

beats.feedback: Good first stops pair the behavior area with its representative tests. Generated CLI Reference is refreshed through the documentation workflow when public command behavior or help changes; it is not the first hand-editing target.

beats.title: Docs are part of the map
beats.text: Use README for product purpose, prerequisites, install, quickstart, and examples. Use docs/onboarding.md for the repo map and working loop. Use docs/developer-guide.md for architecture, testing strategy, command-change workflow, and Definition of Done. Use docs/cli-reference.md for the generated command surface. Use issues/sand/CONTEXT.md before naming user-facing behavior. Use ADRs for durable design decisions, especially the durable Sandbox Spec versus Ephemeral Spec boundary.

beats.title: Tests are behavior landmarks
beats.text: Tests/SandCoreTests maps to behavior areas: CLICommandRouterTests for command shapes and help, SandboxSpecTests for Sandbox Spec rules, FolderPolicyTests for Allowed Folders, WorkingDirectoryMapperTests for cwd mapping, LifecycleCoordinatorTests for normal Sandbox VM flows, EphemeralRunCoordinatorTests for bounded runs, DoctorChecksTests for prerequisites, BackendErrorTranslationTests for user-facing backend failures, and ArchitectureBoundaryTests for dependency and language boundaries. Start with representative tests before changing behavior.

beats.title: Completion gates
beats.text: The Makefile gives the local gates. swift test runs the SandCore XCTest suite. make docs-check runs scripts/docs-check.sh, the Documentation Freshness Gate for generated or managed docs. make check runs swift test and then make docs-check, so it is the normal local completion gate. Use targeted tests while exploring, but a maintainer should know that make check is the repo-level done signal.

beats.instruction: For each hypothetical task, choose the first source area and first test file to inspect. Task 1: duplicate Guest Paths are accepted when adding Allowed Folders. Task 2: sand demo run pwd starts in the wrong Guest Path when the Host Mac cwd is inside an Allowed Folder. Task 3: sand ephemeral init --stdout appears to create Host Metadata even though it should only print a starter spec.
beats.copyText:
Task 1 source: ____
Task 1 test: ____

Task 2 source: ____
Task 2 test: ____

Task 3 source: ____
Task 3 test: ____
signsOfSuccess: Task 3 points first to Sources/SandCore/CLI/CLICommandRouter.swift and Tests/SandCoreTests/CLICommandRouterTests.swift, with Ephemeral sources only if execution behavior is implicated.
No answer starts by editing generated docs or changing files before finding the owner.
hints: Match the noun in the symptom to the repo map: Allowed Folder, Working Directory Mapping, Ephemeral init.
Prefer the smallest owning module before jumping to LifecycleCoordinator.

beats.recap: You can now read the repo as a maintainer map: Package.swift shows products and targets; main.swift wires the executable; Sources/SandCore is split by behavior responsibility; Tests/SandCoreTests mirrors those responsibilities; docs define public contracts and vocabulary; Makefile defines the local gates. The next step is tracing one durable command through that map.
beats.readyPrompt: Given a user-facing behavior, can you name one source area, one test file, one documentation source, and the completion gate you would use before calling the work done?

referenceNeeds: cheat-sheet: repo map from behavior terms to first source area, representative test, and relevant docs
cheat-sheet: local gates summary for swift test, make docs-check, and make check
practice-bank: additional symptom-to-file mapping drills for CLI, lifecycle, folder policy, specs, working directory, and ephemeral init
